response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but the role demands a C++ specialist with industrial automation and IoT expertise. His C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments over 20 years ago and a bachelor thesis project; it does not appear in his listed skills at all. There is no evidence of multi-threading, MQTT, message broker architectures in an industrial context, IEC 62443, SDL practices, or industrial protocols such as Modbus or OPC UA. The candidate's strengths lie entirely outside the core requirements of this position.
strengths: Extensive software craftsmanship background with TDD, CI/CD, and agile practices across 30+ years
Demonstrated event-driven and distributed systems experience (Sociomantic micro-services, GISCollective geo-data platform)
Familiarity with Node.js, React, and cloud-adjacent tooling, which partially match the advantageous skills listed
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — last used substantively over 20 years ago and absent from the skills section entirely
No industrial automation, IoT, or embedded systems background relevant to the role's domain (IEC 62443, Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT are all absent)
No evidence of secure development lifecycle (SDL) practices, industrial cybersecurity standards, or UML/architecture documentation in an industrial context
